projects.title: Seagull in Flight
projects.description: A distant gull crossing the sky as a single confident M-shape. Practice committing to one expressive gesture line.

projects.title: Mooring Bollard
projects.description: A sturdy iron post used to tie up boats. Practice clean vertical construction with a rounded cap on top.

projects.title: Life Preserver Ring
projects.description: A classic ring buoy with rope wraps. Practice finding the center axis and committing to two clean concentric circles.

projects.title: Rope Coil
projects.description: A loose coil of rope on the dock. Practice letting lines wobble and follow the curve of a winding form.

projects.title: Crab
projects.description: A small crab seen from above. Practice building a creature from a wide shell and a handful of small legs.

projects.title: Starfish
projects.description: A five-armed starfish at the water's edge. Practice radial construction around a central point with friendly asymmetry.

projects.title: Distant Lighthouse
projects.description: A small lighthouse on the far headland. Practice stacking simple shapes around a central axis to build a structure.

projects.title: Moored Sailboat
projects.description: A small sailboat at rest on calm water. Practice finding the hull's gesture and fitting a tall sail to its mast.

projects.title: Fisherman's Lantern
projects.description: An old-fashioned glass lantern with a curved handle. Practice keeping junctions clean where the cap, body, and handle all meet.

projects.title: Wicker Creel
projects.description: A woven fishing basket with a shoulder strap. Practice suggesting wicker texture with a few well-placed marks rather than every weave.

projects.title: Anchor
projects.description: A classic anchor with shank, stock, and curved arms. Practice drawing through the form so it feels solid in three dimensions.

projects.title: Fishing Rod
projects.description: A long fishing rod leaning across the dock. Practice drawing a single long confident line from the shoulder.

projects.title: Perched Pelican
projects.description: A hunched pelican settled on the dock. Practice capturing a strong gesture and suggesting feathers without rendering every one.

projects.title: Fishing Net
projects.description: A heaped fishing net with floats and weights. Practice restraint — a few suggested mesh patches read better than every knot drawn.

projects.title: Rowboat
projects.description: A wooden rowboat resting against the dock. Practice reading angles carefully and stopping back lines where the dock crosses in front.

projects.title: Coiled Chain
projects.description: A heavy iron chain piled on the dock. Practice interlocking forms — stopping the back link cleanly where the front link crosses it.

projects.title: Bucket with Catch
projects.description: A bucket of freshly caught fish sitting on the dock. Practice anchoring an object with a cast shadow and varying line weight to add depth.

projects.title: Heron Wading
projects.description: A tall heron standing motionless in the shallows. Practice carrying a long elegant S-curve through head, neck, and body, with multiple mode switches for water and feathers.

projects.title: Apple
projects.description: An apple with a stem and a single leaf. A friendly intro to organic, slightly asymmetric shapes.

projects.title: Pear
projects.description: A pear with a gentle S-curve through its body. Practice finding a subject's gesture and reading how its curves change along the way.

projects.title: Lemon
projects.description: A small lemon with two soft nubs at the ends. A quick study in tilted ovals and suggesting texture with just a few marks.

projects.title: Potted Herb
projects.description: A small herb plant in a clay pot. Practice finding the central axis of a symmetric pot and suggesting bushy leaves without drawing every one.

projects.title: Glass Bottle with Flower
projects.description: A slender glass bottle holding a single flower. Practice finding the axis of a tall symmetric form and keeping clean junctions where stem meets rim.

projects.title: Ceramic Crock
projects.description: A round ceramic crock holding kitchen utensils. Practice symmetric curves around a central axis and reading where a curve tightens versus loosens.

projects.title: Teacup with Saucer
projects.description: A small teacup resting on its saucer. Practice nested ellipses and landing a delicate handle cleanly against the cup's body.

projects.title: Open Book
projects.description: An open book lying flat on the counter. Practice noticing real angles versus assumed ones and curving the page edges to suggest a soft bow.

projects.title: Flower Vase
projects.description: A tall vase with two or three flowers leaning out at gentle angles. Practice symmetric curves, clean stem junctions, and using overlap where stems cross.

projects.title: Honey Jar with Dipper
projects.description: A squat honey jar with a wooden dipper resting in the rim. Practice the overlap where the dipper crosses in front of the jar's mouth.

projects.title: Folded Napkin
projects.description: A folded cloth napkin lying near the counter's edge with one corner drooping forward. Practice reading subtle angles and using line weight and restraint to suggest soft fabric.

projects.title: Terracotta Pot
projects.description: A large planted pot anchoring the courtyard — a friendly first lesson in finding an axis and drawing a symmetrical form.

projects.title: Watering Can
projects.description: A classic galvanized watering can with a long spout — practice clean junctions where spout and handle meet the body.

projects.title: Cut Sunflower
projects.description: A sunflower lying across the tiles — a chance to find a horizontal axis and let petals fall a little unevenly.

projects.title: Butterfly
projects.description: A small butterfly at rest near the tiles — a quick study in symmetry around a vertical axis.

projects.title: Climbing Rose
projects.description: Roses draping over the back wall — practice finding the gesture of a meandering vine and letting blossoms cluster naturally.

projects.title: Stone Birdbath
projects.description: A weathered stone birdbath with three distinct sections — a careful study in axis, curves, and clean junctions.

projects.title: Olive Tree in a Pot
projects.description: A leaning olive tree in a small pot — practice capturing a twisting gesture and suggesting feathery foliage with restraint.

projects.title: Wicker Basket with Produce
projects.description: A handled basket spilling over with fruit — a lesson in curves, overlap, and suggesting weave texture with a few marks.

projects.title: Garden Gate
projects.description: An iron gate set into the back wall — practice noticing real angles and drawing long straight runs from the shoulder.

projects.title: Frog
projects.description: A small frog perched on the tiles — practice finding the gesture, blocking the big shapes, and balancing texture against quiet areas.

projects.title: Sleeping Cat
projects.description: A cat curled asleep in the foreground — the showpiece of the scene, asking for a strong gesture, careful overlap, and a confident hand that knows when to stop.

projects.title: Park Bench
projects.description: A classic slatted park bench — practice building a simple structure from horizontal and vertical lines, and commit to clean junctions where the legs meet the seat.

projects.title: Distant Figure
projects.description: A single person seen from across the park — just a small silhouette. Practice committing to a minimal gesture and resisting the urge to over-detail a tiny form.

projects.title: Seated Figure
projects.description: A figure resting on the park bench. Practice the L-shaped gesture of a seated body and the way the head tips slightly forward in repose.

projects.title: Balloon on a String
projects.description: A round balloon drifting on a long string held by a figure below. Practice a confident curved outline and a flowing line that suggests tension without going rigid.

projects.title: Figure in Mid-Stride
projects.description: A person caught mid-step on the path. Learn to capture the counterbalancing sway of walking — shoulders opposing hips, arms swinging opposite the lead foot.

projects.title: Running Child
projects.description: A child sprinting across the grass, all limbs and momentum. Practice a strong diagonal action line and the wide leg split of a running figure.

projects.title: Dog Walker
projects.description: A person and their dog connected by a taut leash. Practice drawing two gestures that relate to each other, and use overlap to show which subject is in front.

projects.title: Figure Pushing a Stroller
projects.description: A caregiver behind a pram, lower body partially hidden by the frame. Practice using overlap to imply depth, and draw a mechanical object that relates in scale to the human beside it.

projects.title: Couple on a Bench
projects.description: Two figures sharing the bench, their proximity telling a quiet story. Practice managing two gestures in the same space, comparing scale and proportion as you go.

projects.title: Figure Under a Tree
projects.description: A person standing or leaning against a tree trunk, partially wrapped in its shadow. Practice placing a figure within a larger organic form and using overlap to anchor them in the scene.

projects.title: Picnic Group
projects.description: Three or four figures arranged around a blanket in the foreground — the scene's centrepiece. Plan composition first, work background to foreground, and edit ruthlessly before the last ink pass.

projects.title: Classic Storefront Door
projects.description: A four-panel wooden door set in a simple frame with a single step. Practice keeping vertical lines truly vertical and building a grid of rectangles within a rectangle.

projects.title: Shop Window
projects.description: A large plate-glass display window with a low platform and a few suggested items inside. Practice framing a rectangle precisely and implying contents with minimal marks.

projects.title: Street Lamp
projects.description: A classic cast-iron street lamp with a curving arm and a lantern head. Practice drawing a long true vertical and a graceful shepherd's-crook curve branching from it.

projects.title: Fire Hydrant
projects.description: A squat hydrant with a domed cap, side bolt stubs, and a wide base. Practice building a simple symmetrical form and stopping each part's outline cleanly where another part sits over it.

projects.title: Striped Awning
projects.description: A fabric awning sloping out from the building in gentle perspective, with evenly-spaced stripes that narrow toward the far end. Practice parallel lines in perspective and a decorative scalloped valance.

projects.title: Folding Café Chair
projects.description: A bistro chair viewed at a slight angle — all wire-frame geometry. Practice a clean seat quadrilateral, splayed legs that feel grounded, and T-junctions where the back uprights rise from the rear legs.

projects.title: Corner Mailbox
projects.description: A freestanding postal collection box — rounded dome on a rectangular body on a narrow pedestal. Practice drawing through a form to get a symmetrical arch, then inking part by part.

projects.title: Two-Story Facade
projects.description: A full building face — storefront zone below, window grid above, cornice at the top. Practice dividing a large rectangle into smaller ones while keeping one-point perspective consistent.

projects.title: Corner Building
projects.description: A building seen at its corner, two facade planes angling away to separate vanishing points. Set the corner edge first, then let everything else angle from it.

projects.title: Fire Escape
projects.description: A multi-level iron fire escape clinging to a brick wall — horizontal platforms, vertical posts, diagonal stair runs. Practice keeping a geometric structure legible in perspective.

projects.title: Full Streetfront Block
projects.description: Four or five storefronts receding in one-point perspective — the scene's centrepiece. Plan with a horizon line and vanishing point, vary each building's character, and edit to one strong detail per storefront.
